2.1_object_scaling.result: No crossover found. SA maintains IP=1.000 at all N. PT degrades slightly: 0.9984(N=5) to 0.9977(N=15)
2.1_object_scaling.go_nogo_criterion: Clear crossover N* or PT IP advantage at N>=15
2.1_object_scaling.go_nogo_reason: No crossover N* found. SA maintains perfect IP at all object counts. PT gap remains ~0.2% across all N.

2.2_sequence_scaling.result: Gap narrows but PT never surpasses SA. At T=1000: PT=0.9991, SA=0.9998, advantage=-0.07%
2.2_sequence_scaling.go_nogo_criterion: PT IP at T=1000 > SA IP at T=1000 by >= 2%
2.2_sequence_scaling.go_nogo_reason: PT advantage at T=1000 is -0.07%, not >= 2%. SA degrades slightly at long T but still outperforms PT.

2.3_occlusion_recovery.result: PT recovers in 0-1 frames (tau_r << 20). SA maintains perfect IP throughout occlusion.
2.3_occlusion_recovery.go_nogo_reason: tau_r = 0-1 frames across all 7 seeds. PT recovers phase lock almost instantly after occlusion ends.

2.4_velocity_stress.result: PT degrades significantly at high velocities. SA remains perfect.
2.4_velocity_stress.key_finding: PT IP drops to 0.899 at 10x speed while SA maintains 1.000. Phase-based tracking is vulnerable to fast motion.

2.5_noise_sweep.conditions: sigma={0.0-1.0, 13 levels} x 7 seeds x 2 models
2.5_noise_sweep.result: PT degrades exponentially (sigma_c=4.17). SA maintains perfect 1.000 at all noise levels.
2.5_noise_sweep.key_finding: SA is completely noise-invariant. PT shows exponential degradation with characteristic noise scale sigma_c=4.17.

2.6_chimera_nsweep.result: BC=0.50-0.58, order parameter r decreases with N (0.118 to 0.043)
2.6_chimera_nsweep.key_finding: Chimera states confirmed at all system sizes. Order parameter decreasing with N indicates more complex chimera patterns.

go_nogo_summary.paper_implication: Per roadmap: paper is a clean parameter-efficiency story. PT achieves 99.8% of SA IP with 17x fewer parameters. Occlusion recovery (tau_r < 1 frame) adds a self-healing oscillatory binding contribution. Venue: consider ICML/AISTATS over NeurIPS.
go_nogo_summary.honest_assessment: SA is consistently superior in IP across all experimental conditions. PT advantage is purely in parameter efficiency (4,991 vs 83,904 params, 17x reduction) and fast occlusion recovery. The phase-based representation does not provide tracking accuracy advantages over slot attention.

cumulative_with_phase1.combined_narrative: PT and SA are near-equivalent but not identical. SA has a consistent ~0.2% IP advantage. PT compensates with 17x parameter efficiency and instant occlusion recovery.
